English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Laboratory for Elementary Particle Physics records
Cornell University. Laboratory for Elementary Particle Physics (LEPP)
14-22-3898
31 cubic feet. 31 cubic feet.
1953-2010. Printed Materials, Publications Includes publications written by Cornell physicists and colliding beam experiment reports. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1954 ======= 1954 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 7187435
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
June 2011
EAD encoding:
Daniel W. Young, June 2011
Date modified:
Kristen Reichenbach, May 2018
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes publications written by Cornell physicists and colliding beam experiment reports.
RELATED MATERIAL
See also collection #14-22-3483, Laboratory for Elementary Particle Physics Photographs
See also https://www.classe.cornell.edu/public/README.html, LEPP Web Site
See also https://www.classe.cornell.edu/Research/CESR/WebHome.html, CESR Homepage
See also https://www.classe.cornell.edu/Research/CLEO/, CLEO Homepage
SERIES LIST
Series I. Colliding Beam Experiment Reports (CBX) Boxes 1-8
Series II: Colliding Beam Notes (CBN) Boxes 19-21
Series III: Cornell Lab of Nuclear Studies Notes (CLNS) Boxes 8-19, 22-24
Series IV: CSN Boxes 21,22
Series V: Cornell Electron Storage Ring (CESR/CLEO) Proposals, Reports, Presentations Boxes 10, 22, 24, 25, 27-31
Series VI: Energy Recovery Linac (ERL) Boxes 25, 27
Series VII: National Science Foundation (NSF) Boxes 26, 27, 29
Series VIII: Other Proposals, Reports, Manuals, Memos, and Articles Boxes 24, 25, 27, 29, 31
Series IX: Conferences, Workshops Boxes 10, 22-25, 27, 29, 31
Cite As:
Laboratory for Elementary Particle Physics records, #14-22-3898. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
The F.R. Newman Laboratory for Elementary-Particle Physics (LEPP) is a research laboratory of the Cornell University Department of Physics engaged primarily in experimental and theoretical Elementary Particle Physics (EPP) and Accelerator Physics. The primary source of support for LEPP research is the National Science Foundation. LEPP is housed in two main buildings: F.R. Newman Laboratory houses faculty offices, LEPP management, the LEPP theory group, the SuperConducting RF group, machine shop and drafting shop. R. R. Wilson Synchrotron Laboratory houses the CESR storage ring and support staff, the CLEO detector and CLEO collaborator offices, the CHESS facility, and the LNS electronics shop.
Biographical / Historical
Buried 40 feet beneath Alumni Field on the Cornell University campus is the 768 meter Cornell Electron Storage Ring (CESR). CESR is an electron-positron collider operating at a center-of-mass energy in the range of 3.5-12 GeV. Its performance builds on Cornell.s longstanding program in accelerator physics: Cornell built a cyclotron as early as 1935. CESR, completed in 1979, stores beams accelerated by the Cornell Synchrotron, an earlier machine used for fixed target experiments. Using superconducting radio-frequency cavities and pretzel orbits (both technologies developed at Cornell), CESR operated throughout the 1990.s as the highest luminosity electron-positron collider in the world. CLEO-c is a dedicated program of charm physics at the Cornell Electron Storage Ring (CESR).
Superconducting Super Collider. Particle accelerators -- Construction. Nuclear physics -- Research. Physicists. Cornell University. Department of Physics Wilson Synchrotron Laboratory (Cornell University)
